subject: fix(tests): repair 8 pre-existing failures surfaced after npm install
body:
None of the 23 tests added in 3a60a28 had ever been executed — its
session notes said tests were "not yet executed" because npm install
wasn't available in that env. Running `npm install && npm test` here
surfaced 8 failures across two suites, all shipping to main.

IconCacheDisclosure (6 failures): react-test-renderer is deprecated
on React 19. `TestRenderer.create()` returns a renderer that's
unmounted before the test can read `.root`, and the environment
wasn't flagged as act-compatible. Fix: set
IS_REACT_ACT_ENVIRONMENT=true and wrap every create() in act() via
a renderComponent() helper. react-test-renderer is still functional
inside act(), so no dep change needed. Added @types/react-test-renderer
(was silently missing — `typeof import('react-test-renderer')` was
resolving to any, suppressing TS7006 checks on findAll callbacks).

inject-icon-hashes (2 failures):

1. Idempotent re-run emitted a WARNING treating "0 replacements" as
   format drift, even when HTML was already versioned. Script now
   tracks whether HTML contains already-versioned refs for known
   icons and only warns when neither bare nor versioned matches —
   the actual drift case. The existing "format drift" test still
   passes because its fixture has neither.
2. "Missing precached icon" test tried to `rmSync` favicon.png, but
   favicon was dropped from the pipeline in 19821c4 and isn't in the
   fixture. Switched to icon-192.png (still in SW_EXPECTED_URLS).

Unrelated to the migration-sync work on this branch — but surfaced
once `npm install` exposed the test runner, and fixing them here
rather than opening another branch avoids leaving main on a
chronically-red suite.

Tags: testing, pwa, react19, dependencies
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: db-sync
Semver: patch
